source_jobs.title: Founding Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Location: San Francisco, CA

Type: Full-time, on-site

TLDR: we are seeing a surge in market demand as agents become real first-class citizens of the internet, and we are aggressively hiring in order to keep up. We're coming up with solutions in a problem space where no one in Silicon Valley knows the answer to either: how to build something agents want. If you like owning things 0 -> 1, lets chat.

AgentMail is building an API designed to give inboxes to AI agents and be the sole identity + communication provider. It isn't AI for your email, it's email for your AI.

We’re looking for a founding engineer with strong backend and infra instincts to help build the primitives agents will use to communicate, authenticate, and operate in the real world. This is a role for someone who likes systems work: APIs, distributed infrastructure, reliability, developer tooling.

Company highlights:

 - World-Class Team: Founded by a team of experts in their own respects(Quant, SWE, Private Equity), the team has top-tier talent from startups and big companies alike, with backgrounds from Optiver, NVIDIA, Amazon, Modern Treasury, KKR.

 - Hypergrowth: We’ve doubled our user count every 2 weeks since January, and don’t plan on slowing down any time soon. As agents will outnumber humans very soon, we see this multiple changing in the range of orders very soon.

 - Competitive-edge: We are the sole identity provider for agents. Just like you have a Gmail, agent’s will need an AgentMail.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Design APIs and abstractions that are intuitive for both humans and AI Agents

 - Build high-performant, low-latency systems with the idea that agents are the end-users in mind

 - Get your hands dirty by touching our core infrastructure that processes hundreds of thousands of emails per day.

 - Think creatively about being the bridge between cutting edge AI capabilities with something as primitive as email(an oxymoron we know)

 - Own projects end-to-end, from system design to production rollout

 - Write clear, maintainable code and strong technical documentation

 - Be the frontline for feedback from our customers - we’ll trust you to be our spokesperson


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - You want to solve problems end-to-end: every engineer owns a part of the product and needs to make decisions across the frontend, infrastructure, backend, and UX.

 - You have a high bar for quality: At a startup, speed is the only advantage we have over big-tech companies. But even more important is to care about your output and obsess about details.

 - You are a clear thinker and communicator: We have daily standups and weekly syncs in which we expect you to communicate tradeoffs, problems, and blockers directly.

 - You are highly technical: preferred you have prior experience shipping production software by real users as a software engineer at a tech company or startup. But hustlers are always appreciated.


BONUS POINTS

 - Experience with cloud infrastructure, distributed systems, or developer tools

 - Experience with messaging, email, auth, or protocol-heavy systems

 - Experience building for AI/LLM use cases


COMPENSATION

 - $150k - $250k base

 - 0.5% - 1.5% equity

 - Medical, dental, and vision insurance

 - All employees get Equinox memberships

 - Breakfast, lunch, and dinner (Free Doordash)

 - Has architected software used in production by millions of users


COMPENSATION:

 - Breakfast, lunch, and dinner (Doordash)

 - Medical, dental, and vision insurance

 - Equinox membership

source_jobs.title: Founding Revenue Lead
source_jobs.description:
Location: San Francisco, CA

Type: Full-time, on-site


COMPANY OVERVIEW

AgentMail is building the communication and identity layer for AI agents.

Agents are starting to do real work, but they still lack basic infrastructure humans already have: persistent identity, reliable communication, permissions, memory, and a system of record. AgentMail gives every AI agent its own programmable inbox.

Developers use our API to create inboxes, send and receive email, manage threads, parse messages into structured data, route webhooks, attach domains, and build agents that can communicate across the internet.

We're backed by Y Combinator and have raised $6M led by General Catalyst, with support from the founders of Ramp, Supabase, and HubSpot, as well as Paul Graham.

We're early, moving fast, and building in a category without a playbook. If you want to help define core infrastructure for the agent era, we'd love to meet you.


TLDR

We're hiring a Founding Revenue Lead to turn AgentMail's market pull into a repeatable revenue motion. This is not a traditional AE role. You'll help define the category, explain a technical product to different buyers, build the first revenue operating system, and turn customer conversations into sales, product, and positioning strategy.


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own qualification, follow-up, POCs, expansion paths, and early enterprise opportunities

 - Run sales cycles with technical founders, developers, operators, and enterprise buyers

 - Build the first revenue system: CRM hygiene, account plans, follow-up discipline, pipeline stages, weekly revenue reviews

 - Turn customer conversations into ICP, messaging, pricing, packaging, and product learnings

 - Create sales collateral, enterprise decks, case studies, vertical narratives, and objection handling

 - Build upsell paths from self-serve and startup customers into larger deployments

 - Help define the category around agent identity and agent communication infrastructure

 - Work directly with founders on strategic accounts while making revenue less founder-dependent over time


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Early GTM experience at a technical B2B company: devtools, infra, APIs, AI infra, security, identity, data, or workflow automation

 - You can personally sell, not just manage or strategize

 - You can explain a technical product simply to a developer, VP Engineering, operator, and CFO

 - You have strong product marketing instincts: positioning, narrative, decks, use cases, proof points

 - You are organized enough to make sure great accounts do not die from bad follow-up

 - You are hungry, direct, high-agency, and comfortable operating without a polished playbook

 - You want to help invent a market, not just run someone else's process


BONUS POINTS

 - You've built PLG-to-sales or founder-led sales motions before

 - You've sold infrastructure or API products

 - You've worked with usage-based pricing, technical POCs, or enterprise procurement

 - You've owned HubSpot, Salesforce, Attio, Item, or another CRM/revenue system

 - You've written strong GTM collateral yourself

source_jobs.title: Product & PLG Lead
source_jobs.description:
Location: San Francisco, CA

Type: Full-time, on-site


COMPANY OVERVIEW

AgentMail is building the communication and identity layer for AI agents.

Agents are starting to do real work, but they still lack basic infrastructure humans already have: persistent identity, reliable communication, permissions, memory, and a system of record. AgentMail gives every AI agent its own programmable inbox.

Developers use our API to create inboxes, send and receive email, manage threads, parse messages into structured data, route webhooks, attach domains, and build agents that can communicate across the internet.

We're backed by Y Combinator and have raised $6M led by General Catalyst, with support from the founders of Ramp, Supabase, and HubSpot, as well as Paul Graham.

We're early, moving fast, and building in a category without a playbook. If you want to help define core infrastructure for the agent era, we'd love to meet you.


TLDR

We're hiring a Product & PLG Lead to understand who uses AgentMail, why they need it, where they get stuck, what makes them activate, and how product usage turns into expansion.

This role owns the product-led growth loop. You'll work across PostHog, customer calls, onboarding flows, docs, API usage, lifecycle emails, and experiments. Your job is to understand what people are building with AgentMail, which use cases matter most, and how to get more users to value faster.


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own product analytics across PostHog and related tools

 - Build dashboards for signup quality, activation, retention, expansion, churn, and PLG-to-sales conversion

 - Segment users by persona, use case, company type, behavior, API usage, and revenue potential

 - Run A/B tests across onboarding, docs, lifecycle emails, pricing, packaging, and activation flows

 - Identify which API features and use cases drive the strongest retention and expansion

 - Interview customers and translate usage into clear personas and product insights

 - Build lifecycle campaigns that help users get to value faster

 - Partner with engineering to improve docs, examples, templates, SDK flows, and first-run experience

 - Partner with revenue to identify high-intent accounts and expansion opportunities


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - You are obsessive about funnels, product behavior, event tracking, and user intent

 - You have worked on PLG, growth, product, analytics, or lifecycle at a technical product company

 - You know tools like PostHog, Amplitude, Mixpanel, Segment, Customer.io, SQL, Hex, Metabase, or similar

 - You can form a hypothesis, instrument it, ship an experiment, and interpret the result

 - You care about developer experience and business outcomes

 - You can talk to users and also inspect raw product data

 - You notice tiny product and messaging details that others miss

 - You are comfortable with ambiguity and can create structure from messy usage patterns


BONUS POINTS

 - Experience with developer tools, APIs, infra, AI products, or B2B PLG

 - Experience turning self-serve users into sales-qualified accounts

 - Experience with pricing/packaging experiments

 - Experience building onboarding, docs, lifecycle, or activation systems

 - You can write clear product copy and explain technical concepts simply

notes: Early-stage startup with a strong focus on engineering and product development.
